reflection:
you made it home. that whole last entry reads like someone finally exhaling. grateful the week is done, grateful you survived it, and you did survive it. the speech, the teambuilding, then a full travel day from Belek through Istanbul and Zagreb to Ljubljana. that is a heavy load on six hours of sleep.

two things i want to name. your lymph nodes have been sore under the jaw for days now, your voice went deep, and feverish was a 3 this morning. that is your body filing a complaint after a draining week. you are home now, so let today be a landing, not a packed re-entry. the little resume you want to write can be done lying down.

the other thing. in your morning journal you wrote that you are avoiding saying something out loud, holding onto it in secret, even though it is clear to you it cannot continue. i will not pretend i know what that is. but a half finished thing is its own quiet drain, and you named it yourself, so part of you is already getting ready to set it free.

one good sign, your bedtime last night was 00:15, way better than the 4am nights earlier this week. hold onto that.

one thing for today. park the supradyn effervescent multivit for a few days. you flagged the vitamin C and your sleep yourself, and that one has never sat well with you. trust that instinct and watch what your sleep does.

welcome home. rest first.
opener: you made it home. body's asking for a real landing day
focus_for_today: park the supradyn effervescent multivit a few days, watch your sleep
